net.bhl.cdt.paxelerate.model.astar

Class Costmap

  • java.lang.Object
    • net.bhl.cdt.paxelerate.model.astar.Costmap

  • public class Costmap
extends java.lang.Object
    This class creates a cost map by flooding the whole map from a specific point. Every element of the map has its own cost value, during flooding they are summed up. So every value of the finished cost map represents the total cost to get there from the initial value. In its current configuration, the cost map is fed with an area map, width, height and the initial starting point.
    Since:
    0.5
    Version:
    1.3
    Author:
    marc.engelmann
    See Also:
    net.bhl.cdt.model.astar/AreaMap.java, net.bhl.cdt.model.astar/Node.java

      • Costmap

        public Costmap(Vector size,
               Vector start,
               Areamap areamap,
               Agent agent,
               boolean OnlyFloodToSeat)
        Instantiates a new costmap.
        Parameters:
        size - the size
        start - the start
        areamap - the areamap
        agent - the agent
        OnlyFloodToSeat - the only flood to seat

    • Method Detail

      • setCost

        public void setCost(Vector position,
                    int value)
        Sets the cost.
        Parameters:
        position - the position
        value - the value

      • printMapToConsole

        public void printMapToConsole()
        This method prints the cost map with values.

      • printMapPathToConsole

        public void printMapPathToConsole(Path path,
                                  Areamap areamap,
                                  Agent agent)
        This method prints the cost map with a path to the console.
        Parameters:
        path - the path
        areamap - the areamap
        agent - the agent

      • getCost

        public int getCost(Vector point)
        This method returns the already calculated cost of a specific point in the cost map.
        Parameters:
        point - is the point (of dimension int[2]) of interest
        Returns:
        the cost at the specific point

      • getMaximumCost

        public int getMaximumCost()
        Gets the maximum cost.
        Returns:
        the maximum cost

      • getSurroundingPoints

        public java.util.ArrayList<Vector> getSurroundingPoints(int x,
                                                        int y)
        This method delivers all 8 surrounding points of a specific point in the cost map. Starting in the north, all points are collected clockwise.
        Parameters:
        x - x coordinate of the middle point
        y - y coordinate of the middle point
        Returns:
        returns the point vector
